title: Intense Smoky Japanese Brussels Sprouts Nabemono
description: A smoky, cheesy, one-pot Japanese dish featuring Brussels sprouts, inspired by nabemono and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly principles, with a papillote twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the Brussels sprouts and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add the sake, mirin, and soy sauce, and stir to combine.
Cover the pot with parchment paper, creating a papillote, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until the Brussels sprouts are tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the smoked gouda cheese until melted and creamy.
Season with smoked paprika and stir to combine.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped green onions.
